title: Light Fluffy Argentinian Beef Empanadas
description: A malty, fluffy, and comforting Argentinian dish featuring tamarind paste and lardons, perfect for a snack or light meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, malt powder, and salt.
Add the beef broth and egg to the bowl, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a tablespoon of tamarind paste and a few pieces of lardon in the center of each circle.
Top with a tablespoon of ground beef and fold the dough in half to form a half moon shape.
Brush the tops with a little bit of water and bake for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
